seoTitle: Port Franklin vs Port Melbourne — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Franklin and Port Melbourne. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Franklin vs Port Melbourne: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Franklin and Port Melbourne.
heading: Port Franklin vs Port Melbourne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Franklin, Victoria and Port Melbourne, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Franklin has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Port Melbourne (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Franklin or Port Melbourne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Franklin has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Port Melbourne scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Franklin scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Port Melbourne scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Franklin or Port Melbourne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Franklin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
